items.title: A narrow developmental window defines PKN2’s essential role in ventricular chamber morphogenesis
items.author: Ju Chen
items.content: Multi-omic and quantitative morphometric analyses define a narrow early developmental window during which cardiomyocytes require PKN2 for ventricular chamber morphogenesis in the mouse heart.

items.title: Oscillatory network efficiency predicts mood and fatigue during sleep deprivation
items.author: William D. S. Killgore
items.content: Efficiency of regional networks in frontal, limbic, and sensorimotor regions show unique oscillatory patterns of reorganization. Variation in psychomotor vigilance, fatigue, and negative affect reflect dynamic changes in functional network topology.

items.title: Regulation of the PKD2 channel function and associated disease phenotypes by RASSF4
items.author: Xing-Zhen Chen
items.content: RASSF4 associates with PKD2 to enhance its channel function and suppress RAS/MAPK signaling, mitigating polycystic kidney disease phenotypes and highlighting a potential therapeutic target for ADPKD.

items.title: Spatial transcriptomic profiling of developing mouse hearts reveals a spatially patterned signaling environment
items.author: Guang Li
items.content: The authors present spatial transcriptomics atlases of developing mouse hearts at embryonic and neonatal stages, complemented with ligand–receptor interaction analyses.

items.title: Plasmodium falciparum PfMyoF contains a Rab-like tail domain and associates with perinuclear membrane trafficking proteins
items.author: Folma Buss
items.content: PfMyoF is a unique, plus-end-directed Plasmodium falciparum myosin motor with WD40 and Rab-like tail domains. It binds PfCaM, associates with perinuclear membranes and trafficking proteins, and is likely important for blood-stage parasite growth.

items.title: Dynamic uterine microenvironment drives endometrial adenocarcinoma carcinogenesis and progression
items.author: Yusi Fu
items.content: Single-cell profiling of uterine blood reveals neutrophil reprogramming and transcriptomic signatures that track endometrial cancer progression from premalignant lesions to invasive carcinoma.

items.title: Author Correction: EXCRETE workflow enables deep proteomics of the microbial extracellular environment
items.author: Julie A. Z. Zedler
items.content: Author Correction: EXCRETE workflow enables deep proteomics of the microbial extracellular environment

items.title: Atg7–Atg12 conjugation and autophagy are negatively regulated by the disordered region of Atg12
items.author: Daniel J. Klionsky
items.content: Probing thioester-forming recombinant complexes containing Atg8 or Atg12 with either Atg7 alone or Atg7-E2 dimers provides mechanistic insights into thioesterification reactions in autophagy.
